disagreementModel.explanation: The reports are not contradictory. The baseline report measures baseline lanes and is currently red because fixtures-runner fails, while secondary-release-report measures a narrower secondary-surface contract set and explicitly treats live/provider proof plus several planned repo/install recordings as non-blocking or future closure work.

downstreamClosurePlan.expectation: Retire or truthfully replace the blocking fixtures-runner baseline gap so baseline and release reporting no longer disagree on the representative deterministic lane.
Codify whether optional live evidence remains permanently non-blocking or gains a stronger policy requirement, without silently changing release semantics.
Resolve web-mode and worktree/session partial surfaces into either stronger release-readable proof rows or documented scoped exceptions.
Resolve MCP and workflow/BMAD partial surfaces into final parity closure targets so the secondary matrix can move from partial to fully intentional coverage.

rows.title: Deterministic fixture replay lane remains the blocking baseline gap
rows.rationale: The canonical baseline report is red because this deterministic fixture lane exits non-zero. That is not contradicted by the green secondary release gate because the secondary gate does not consume the baseline fixture-replay lane as a required surface.
rows.remediationSummary: Restore truthful fixture-replay pass/fail evidence for tests/fixtures/run.ts, or replace this lane with a more precise deterministic baseline lane that still explains the same planning-to-execution path.
rows.closureExpectation: Closure requires the baseline report to stop failing on the representative deterministic lane rather than merely documenting the failure.

evidencePaths.reason: Canonical baseline report showing fixtures-runner failed.
Failing deterministic fixture-replay lane implementation.
Tracked representative planning-to-execution manifest the baseline report already uses.

rows.title: Provider-driven live lane stays explicit but non-blocking
rows.rationale: The live lane is intentionally opt-in and the secondary release gate records it as optional. A skipped live spot-check therefore preserves observability without blocking release-readable closure for deterministic surfaces.
rows.remediationSummary: Keep the skip reason truthful, or run the lane only when live-provider configuration is intentionally enabled. Do not promote this lane to a release blocker unless downstream policy changes.
rows.closureExpectation: Closure means the optional policy and skip reason stay explicit; a passing live run is useful evidence but is not required for the canonical release gate to pass.

evidencePaths.reason: Canonical baseline report showing the live lane is skipped.
Secondary release gate records live-runner as non-blocking optional evidence.
Opt-in live parity lane implementation.

rows.title: Browser-only web mode parity surface remains partially closed and needs an explicit closure decision
rows.rationale: Baseline parity still records web-mode as partial because release-readable scope gaps remain, while the secondary release gate passes because the currently required secondary lane for this surface is already satisfied. That is a scoped-closure question, not a present release blocker.
rows.remediationSummary: No dedicated secondary-surface parity artifact reports web-mode coverage in the parity release workflow. Current web-mode evidence is integration-heavy but not framed as installed-mode parity proof alongside repo-mode parity.
rows.closureExpectation: Publish a machine-readable lane/report entry so release-gate consumers can read web-mode parity without re-inferring it from individual tests. Define whether web mode needs repo/install dual proof or an explicit scoped exception in the parity matrix.

evidencePaths.reason: Tracked existing evidence for web-mode.

rows.title: MCP server and client parity surface remains partially closed and needs an explicit closure decision
rows.rationale: Baseline parity still records mcp as partial because release-readable scope gaps remain, while the secondary release gate passes because the currently required secondary lane for this surface is already satisfied. That is a scoped-closure question, not a present release blocker.
rows.remediationSummary: No secondary parity lane currently emits MCP-specific covered/uncovered status into a tracked parity artifact. Existing MCP tests do not yet demonstrate the installed packaged CLI serving a representative MCP parity interaction in the parity report surface.
rows.closureExpectation: Add a deterministic MCP parity contract/report lane so downstream slices stop treating MCP support as an implicit side effect of unit tests. Define a representative MCP interaction and attach it to the parity matrix.

rows.title: Workflow / BMAD representative parity surface remains partially closed and needs an explicit closure decision
rows.rationale: Baseline parity still records workflow-bmad as partial because release-readable scope gaps remain, while the secondary release gate passes because the currently required secondary lane for this surface is already satisfied. That is a scoped-closure question, not a present release blocker.
rows.remediationSummary: No tracked parity fixture yet exercises one representative workflow/BMAD path as a release-readable contract. BMAD-relevant workflow coverage exists only indirectly through command/action contracts and shipped workflow assets.
rows.closureExpectation: Define a deterministic workflow fixture or manifest entry so the parity report can state exactly what workflow parity means. Publish explicit scope semantics describing which workflow/BMAD experiences are in or out of parity scope.

rows.title: Worktree, session, and recovery parity surface remains partially closed and needs an explicit closure decision
rows.rationale: Baseline parity still records worktree-session-recovery as partial because release-readable scope gaps remain, while the secondary release gate passes because the currently required secondary lane for this surface is already satisfied. That is a scoped-closure question, not a present release blocker.
rows.remediationSummary: Worktree/session/recovery evidence is scattered across CLI and web tests but not summarized in a parity artifact. There is no deterministic installed-mode parity fixture demonstrating representative worktree/session recovery behavior beyond individual contract tests.
rows.closureExpectation: Publish one machine-readable matrix row that downstream slices and release gates can consume directly. Decide whether a worktree/session fixture is required or whether scoped contract coverage is sufficient for parity closure.
